📋 Current Tariff Rates
As of April 2025 — contact SEI NA Customs team for updates
Base HTS
HTS Base Duty Rate
Per-code · 2025 HTSUS Column 1
The MFN (Most Favored Nation) general rate of duty for each HTS code, sourced from the 2025 Harmonized Tariff Schedule. Ranges from Free to ~13% depending on product category. Pre-loaded for all 1,500+ codes in the parts database.
§ 232 15–50%
Steel · Aluminum · Copper
All countries · Specific HTS codes
Section 232 national security tariffs apply to metal-based imports and are determined by the product's HTS code. Three rate tiers apply: 50% for articles made entirely or almost entirely of steel, aluminum, or copper; 25% for derivative products substantially composed of these metals; and 15% (temporary) for certain metal-intensive equipment. The HTS code determines which tier applies.
§ 122 10%
Universal Baseline
All countries · Non-§232 imports only
A 10% additional tariff applies under Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 to all U.S. imports not already subject to Section 232. Imports covered by §232 are subject to those rates instead — §122 does not stack on top of §232 duties.
§ 301 +25%
China Surcharge
China (CN) only
An additional 25% surcharge applies to all imports from China under Section 301. This surcharge applies on top of both §232 and §122 duties — for Chinese-origin goods, the total additional tariff is §232 + 25% or §122 + 25% depending on the HTS code. Individual HTS exclusions may apply — flag manually.
Total Duty = Base Duty (HTS) + Additional Tariff (§122 or §232) + §301 Surcharge (CN/RU only)
Arrange your data in Excel
Before pasting, ensure your columns are in this exact left-to-right order:
Part Number Description Extended Cost ($) Country of Origin HTS Code (optional)
Country of Origin is the country where the part was manufactured — not where it was shipped from. Tariff liability is determined by origin, not by the country of export. Any column can be left blank. Missing cost fields will prevent duty amounts from calculating (percentages will still show). A missing country of origin means no additional tariff can be calculated for that row.
Paste into the input grid
Click the first cell of the paste grid and press Ctrl+V. Your data will populate in the same arrangement as your Excel file. Review each row to confirm it looks correct before proceeding. You can also type directly into any cell, or use Tab and Enter to navigate between cells.
Click "Load & Lookup"
The tool will send all populated rows to the calculator and automatically look up each part's HTS code and base material from the parts reference database. Results appear in the Parts & Calculations table below.
Review HTS codes
Each row's HTS code is flagged with a colored dot indicating confidence:

● Exact  The part number was found directly in the reference database. No action needed.

● Fuzzy  No exact part number match was found. The tool matched by description similarity and is suggesting an HTS code — you must confirm or reject this suggestion using the ✓ / ✕ buttons before the calculation is finalized.

● Manual / Missing  No match was found. If you left the HTS field blank, the tool cannot calculate base duty for this row. HTS codes can be found in SAP under the Foreign Trade tab of the Material Master. If a code is missing or unknown, submit a classification request to the SEI US Customs team via the CRT tool.
Verify base materials
Section 232 tariffs apply selectively based on what a part is made of (steel, aluminum, or copper) — so accurate material classification directly affects the additional tariff rate calculated. Review the Base Material column for each row and correct any misclassifications using the dropdown.

If the material is listed as — unknown —, the tool will assume whichever material results in the highest possible duty rate for that HTS code. You may leave it unknown as a conservative estimate, or select the correct material for a more accurate result.
Click "Calculate All"
The tool calculates base duty, additional tariff, and total duty impact for every row. A summary with totals and effective rate appears at the bottom of the page. You can recalculate at any time after editing a field — individual rows update automatically when you change a value.
Export your results (optional)
Use the Export CSV or Export Excel buttons at the bottom of the calculator to download all rows with their calculated duty figures, tariff breakdowns, and flags.
